concept: Egg / The Round Case of Beginning
description: The egg: life's sealed, self-contained beginning — the bird's, the fish's, the snake's. With 'thowia' (born) and 'thinoe' (seed) it completes the vocabulary of starts: seed for plants, egg for creatures, birth for the moment itself.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'po' is the shell, and 'we'–'a' opens outward from it — the word hatches as it is spoken.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'pelori wi powea thema.' (The bird guards two eggs.) With classifier: 'ta lipha powea' (one egg, as living) — the egg takes 'lipha', since it is life in waiting.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Potential made visible: everything the bird will be, resting complete and unhurried in the shell. The egg is patience with a future inside.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The egg was the household's most honest measure of a season's health — counted gently, borrowed against never.
